title: Just As I Am
author: Charlotte Elliott

scriptureRefs.note: Him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out
Whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely

story: Charlotte Elliott, an invalid who suffered from chronic illness, wrote this hymn in 1835 after struggling with feelings of uselessness. A visiting minister, Cesar Malan, had told her years earlier to come to Christ 'just as you are.' Those words transformed her life and inspired this hymn. Billy Graham later used it as the standard invitation hymn at his crusades, and it was sung at more of his events than any other song.

lines: Just as I am, without one plea,
But that Thy blood was shed for me,
And that Thou bidst me come to Thee,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
Just as I am, and waiting not
To rid my soul of one dark blot,
To Thee whose blood can cleanse each spot,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
Just as I am, though tossed about
With many a conflict, many a doubt,
Fightings and fears within, without,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
Just as I am, poor, wretched, blind;
Sight, riches, healing of the mind,
Yea, all I need in Thee to find,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
Just as I am, Thou wilt receive,
Wilt welcome, pardon, cleanse, relieve;
Because Thy promise I believe,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
Just as I am, Thy love unknown
Hath broken every barrier down;
Now, to be Thine, yea, Thine alone,
O Lamb of God, I come! I come!
